description_long: This is a stone obelisk monument set on a square, tiered base, likely constructed from weathered granite or similar stone. The obelisk rises from a circular plinth, which is itself supported by a square pedestal with chamfered corners, all resting on a broad, stepped platform. The shaft of the obelisk is tall and tapers to a pointed tip, with visible discoloration and moss growth indicating age and exposure to the elements. One face of the pedestal features an engraved inscription panel, while another side has a recessed square, possibly for a plaque or additional inscription. The overall appearance is solemn and commemorative, suitable for placement in a park, plaza, or cemetery.
description: A weathered stone obelisk monument with a square, tiered base and engraved inscription panel, set on a broad platform. The structure is aged, with visible moss and discoloration, and features a pointed tip.
description_view_0: Front view showing the obelisk's main inscription panel and the circular plinth atop a square, stepped base.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view with the inscription panel still visible, highlighting the weathered surface and moss on the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a recessed square on the pedestal, possibly for a plaque, and the clean lines of the obelisk's shaft.
description_view_3: Side view showing the plain face of the pedestal and the tapering shaft, with less visible ornamentation.
attribute_inscription: There is an engraved inscription panel on the front face of the pedestal, with text that appears weathered and partially illegible. The style is formal, likely in English, and the letters are incised into the stone.
attribute_ornamentation: Minimal ornamentation; the only decorative elements are the recessed inscription panel and a square recess on the rear face. No elaborate carvings or motifs are visible.
attribute_base_design: The base consists of a broad, square, stepped platform supporting a square pedestal with chamfered corners. Above this is a circular plinth from which the obelisk rises. The base is made of the same stone as the obelisk and shows signs of weathering and moss.
attribute_tip_design: The tip of the obelisk is sharply pointed and unadorned, with a simple, clean finish matching the shaft.
